sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Is Hut 8’s AI Data Center Pivot Amid Heavy 2025 Losses Altering The Investment Case For Hut 8 (HUT)?
feed.summary: Hut 8 Corp. reported significant losses in 2025 but announced a strategic pivot towards building AI data center infrastructure through partnerships with Anthropic and Fluidstack. This move aims to diversify revenue away from Bitcoin-linked income and expand its AI/HPC pipeline, though it also introduces execution risks for large-scale projects. The shift seeks to justify its current sales multiple despite ongoing losses, as analyst forecasts vary regarding the success of this capital-intensive strategy.

feed.title: Dycom Industries Reports 2025 Earnings: $5.55B Revenue, Issues 2026 Guidance - News and Statistics
feed.summary: Dycom Industries Inc. has released its fiscal fourth-quarter and full-year 2025 financial results. The company reported a quarterly profit of $16.3 million on revenues of $1.46 billion, and an annual profit of $281.2 million with total revenue reaching $5.55 billion. For the upcoming fiscal year, Dycom anticipates full-year revenue to be between $6.85 billion and $7.15 billion.

feed.title: Chatham Lodging Acquires Six Hilton Properties, Increases Quarterly Dividend
feed.summary: Chatham Lodging Trust (CLDT) has acquired six Hilton-branded hotels for $92 million, comprising 589 rooms, to enhance its portfolio with newer properties and focus on extended-stay guests. This strategic move aims to improve profit margins and increase adjusted funds from operations by approximately $0.10 per share annually. Reflecting confidence, CLDT also raised its quarterly common dividend by 11% to $0.10 per share.

feed.title: Honda to import China-made EVs to bolster Japanese lineup
feed.summary: Honda Motor is set to become the first Japanese automaker to sell China-made electric vehicles in its domestic market, with sales starting as early as this spring. The initiative aims to offer more EV options to Japanese consumers, beginning with models like the Honda e:NP2. This strategic move highlights increasing integration between Chinese manufacturing and the global automotive market.

feed.title: Equifax credit checks: what changed now and why it matters
feed.summary: Equifax is tightening its credit check processes in the US, impacting how lenders, landlords, and employers assess individuals. The article explains these changes, highlights expert warnings, and provides advice on protecting one's credit score. It delves into the mechanics of Equifax credit checks, their expanding role, and offers a consumer playbook for managing credit reports effectively.

feed.title: Valero Energy Corporation Announces Pricing of Notes Offering
feed.summary: Valero Energy Corporation announced the pricing of a public offering of $850 million aggregate principal amount of 5.150% Senior Notes due 2036. The offering is expected to close on March 10, 2026, with net proceeds primarily intended for general corporate purposes, including the repayment or redemption of existing debentures and senior notes. SMBC Nikko Securities America, Inc., Citigroup Global Markets Inc., MUFG Securities Americas Inc., and Wells Fargo Securities, LLC are acting as joint book-running managers for the offering.

feed.title: Mosaic (MOS) Maintains Quarterly Dividend at $0.22/Share
feed.summary: Mosaic Company (MOS) has announced it will maintain its quarterly dividend at $0.22 per share, resulting in a 3.35% forward yield. Despite plans for a significant $1.5 billion capital expenditure by 2026 to boost phosphate and potash production, the company faces challenges including declining revenue growth and profitability, with its Altman Z-Score indicating potential financial stress. Strategic investments aim for long-term growth, but current financial metrics suggest caution for investors.

feed.title: Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) Stock Price Down 1.3% - Here's What Happened
feed.summary: Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ:SMCI) experienced a 1.3% decline in its stock price during trading on Thursday, with the stock closing at $32.24 and 26.8 million shares changing hands. The company has a consensus "Hold" rating from analysts with an average target price of $43.43, following various analyst rating changes and price target adjustments. Super Micro Computer reported strong earnings in its latest quarter, exceeding analyst expectations for EPS and revenue, and institutional investors have recently adjusted their positions in the stock.

feed.title: Paycom Expands Share Repurchase Authorization with Added Funds
feed.summary: Paycom's board has authorized an additional $200 million for its stock repurchase program, following approximately $1.45 billion in buybacks since July 2024. The program, which allows for flexible repurchases and can be suspended at any time, is set to expire on August 15, 2026. TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates PAYC as an "Outperform" due to its financial strength, conservative balance sheet, and strong cash generation, despite weak technical momentum.

feed.title: Food City Partners with Coca-Cola to Launch Sprite Cherry in Stores
feed.summary: Food City, in partnership with Coca-Cola Consolidated and The Coca-Cola Company, is launching an exclusive Sprite Cherry beverage. This new flavor will be available only at Food City locations through September 2026, offering customers a refreshing option for spring and summer. Both companies expressed enthusiasm for the collaboration and the new product's introduction.`

feed.title: RAM Mounts Partners with Tyler Technologies to Support Smarter, More Durable Onboard Student Transportation Technology
feed.summary: RAM Mounts has partnered with Tyler Technologies to provide more durable and reliable onboard technology for student transportation. This collaboration combines Tyler Technologies' student transportation software with RAM Mounts' rugged mounting solutions, ensuring that devices like Samsung enterprise-grade tablets perform reliably in demanding school bus environments. The partnership aims to enhance safety, efficiency, and scalability for school districts by providing secure, adjustable, and vibration-damping mounting options for mobile devices.

feed.title: VICI Properties Keeps Quarterly Dividend at $0.45 a Share, Payable April 9 to Holders of Record March 19
feed.summary: VICI Properties will maintain its quarterly dividend at $0.45 per share. The dividend is payable on April 9, 2026, to shareholders of record as of March 19, 2026. This announcement follows several recent analyst adjustments to VICI Properties' price target.

feed.title: VICI Properties (VICI) Maintains Quarterly Dividend, Offers Stro
feed.summary: VICI Properties (VICI) has announced it will maintain its quarterly dividend at $0.45 per share, resulting in a 6.06% forward yield. The company is strategically targeting $2.625 billion in adjusted funds from operations by 2026 and is diversifying its portfolio within the REIT sector. Despite its strong financial health and positive analyst sentiment, VICI's Altman Z-Score suggests some financial stress, and its debt issuance strategy warrants monitoring.

feed.title: Equinix Issues $1.5 Billion in New Senior Notes
feed.summary: Equinix's Singapore and European financing subsidiaries issued $1.5 billion in senior unsecured notes on March 5, 2026, comprising $700 million at 4.400% due 2031 and $800 million at 4.700% due 2033. Cross-currency swaps were utilized to lower effective interest rates and manage multi-currency funding risks for the company. An AI Analyst from TipRanks rates EQIX as "Outperform" due to strong operating performance and a positive 2026 outlook, despite valuation and financial risk concerns.

feed.title: T Stamp Completes Lexverify Acquisition to Expand AI Capabilities
feed.summary: T Stamp, Inc. (IDAI) has completed the acquisition of U.K.-based Lexverify Ltd., an all-stock transaction aimed at expanding T Stamp's expertise in large language models and strengthening its access to the U.K. market. The acquisition involves safeguards such as non-compete clauses and commitments to maintain employee terms, with the purchase price paid in four tranches of Class A common stock. Despite the acquisition, TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates IDAI as "Neutral" due to weak financial performance and bearish technicals, although corporate updates could provide upside catalysts.

feed.title: Medtronic announces cash dividend for fourth quarter of fiscal year 2026
feed.summary: Medtronic plc announced a cash dividend of $0.71 per ordinary share for the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2026, consistent with an increase announced in May 2025. The company, which is part of the S&P 500 Dividend Aristocrats, has increased its annual dividend for 48 consecutive years. The dividend is payable on April 17, 2026, to shareholders of record by March 27, 2026.

feed.title: INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf of Investors of Hercules Capital, Inc. - HTGC
feed.summary: Pomerantz LLP is investigating Hercules Capital, Inc. (NYSE: HTGC) after a short report by Hunterbrook Media alleged the company marks software debt at 100 cents on the dollar despite industry distress, and that a significant portion of its reported income comes from payment-in-kind loans. Following the report, Hercules's stock price dropped by 7.91%. Investors harmed by potential securities fraud or unlawful business practices are advised to contact the firm.

feed.title: INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf of Investors of Gartner, Inc. - IT
feed.summary: Pomerantz LLP is investigating potential securities fraud claims against Gartner, Inc. (NYSE: IT) on behalf of its investors. This investigation follows Gartner's Q4 2025 financial results and 2026 outlook, where it projected revenue and adjusted earnings below analyst expectations. Following this announcement, Gartner's stock price declined significantly.

feed.title: Costco (NASDAQ:COST) Posts Better-Than-Expected Sales In Q1 CY2026
feed.summary: Costco (NASDAQ:COST) reported Q1 CY2026 results with revenue of $69.6 billion, exceeding analyst expectations by 0.8% and marking a 9.2% year-on-year increase. Diluted EPS also beat estimates, though Adjusted EBITDA missed projections. The company continues to show strong same-store sales growth and store expansion, indicating sustained performance in the consumer retail sector.

feed.title: Insiders Are Buying These 3 Energy Stocks
feed.summary: Insiders have recently purchased shares in three energy companies: Texas Pacific Land (TPL), DT Midstream (DTM), and CMS Energy (CMS). These transactions are often seen by investors as a longer-term indicator, though they should not be relied upon for short-term performance given the differing holding periods and strict rules for insiders. All three companies have shown strong performance in 2026, with CMS and DTM recently raising dividends and TPL exploring data center opportunities.

feed.title: Costco Tops Earnings Forecast on Strong Shopper Demand
feed.summary: Costco Wholesale Corp. reported better-than-expected quarterly profit, driven by strong shopper demand and increased membership fees. The company's profits rose to $4.58 per share, surpassing Bloomberg estimates, and membership fees totaled $1.36 billion. Costco continues to gain market share by offering value and expanding digital services, including partnerships with third-party delivery companies and new offerings like cake delivery, which is attracting a younger demographic.

feed.title: Raymond James Maintains Strong Buy on Waystar Holding Corp (WAY) Mar 2026
feed.summary: On March 5, 2026, Raymond James maintained a "Strong Buy" rating and Bank of America Securities reiterated a "Buy" rating for Waystar Holding Corp (WAY), signaling continued analyst confidence due to Waystar's enterprise positioning and partnerships. No new price targets were issued, and intraday moves for the stock were modest. Meyka AI assigned WAY a B+ grade, reflecting positive performance metrics.

feed.title: Netflix acquires Ben Affleck’s AI tech company InterPositive
feed.summary: Netflix has announced the acquisition of InterPositive, an AI tech company founded by Ben Affleck that creates AI-powered tools for filmmakers. Affleck will also join Netflix as a senior adviser as part of the deal. The company's mission is to protect and expand creative choice using new technology, aligning with Netflix's belief in innovation serving storytellers.

feed.title: INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf of Investors of KKR & Co. Inc. - KKR
feed.summary: Pomerantz LLP is investigating KKR & Co. Inc. on behalf of investors following a significant dividend cut by FS KKR Capital Corp. (FSK). The investigation concerns potential securities fraud after FSK reduced its dividend from $0.70 to $0.48 per share, citing "specific challenges" and non-accrual on 3.4% of its portfolio. This announcement led to a 9.26% drop in KKR's stock price over two trading sessions.

feed.title: P/E Ratio Insights for Super Micro Computer
feed.summary: This article provides insights into Super Micro Computer (NASDAQ: SMCI)'s P/E ratio, noting its current share price, recent performance, and its P/E relative to the Technology Hardware, Storage & Peripherals industry. It explains that a lower P/E ratio can indicate undervaluation but also emphasizes considering other financial metrics and industry trends for comprehensive investment decisions. The piece highlights that while SMCI has a lower P/E than its industry average, investors should not solely rely on this metric.

feed.title: MetLife Confirms First-Quarter 2026 Series A Preferred Dividend
feed.summary: MetLife Inc. confirmed a first-quarter 2026 dividend of $0.31501229 per share on its Series A preferred stock, payable on March 16, 2026, to shareholders of record as of February 27, 2026. This announcement highlights MetLife's consistent capital return policy to preferred shareholders and provides clarity for investors. While analyst ratings are generally positive (Buy with a $93.00 price target), TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates MET as Neutral due to weaker financial quality and bearish technical signals, despite a moderate valuation and positive earnings outlook.

feed.title: American Tower Declares Quarterly Cash Distribution to Shareholders
feed.summary: American Tower (AMT) has declared a quarterly cash distribution of $1.79 per share, payable on April 28, 2026, to shareholders of record as of April 14, 2026. This payout reinforces the company's commitment to returning capital to investors as a leading digital infrastructure REIT. Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, rates AMT as Neutral, citing strong margins and improving free cash flow balanced by leverage and a challenging 2026 outlook.

feed.title: 2 Top Healthcare Dividend Stocks to Buy and Hold Forever
feed.summary: This article identifies Bristol Myers Squibb and Medtronic as two leading healthcare companies offering robust businesses and attractive dividend programs, making them suitable for long-term investment. Despite challenges like patent expirations for Bristol Myers Squibb and strategic changes for Medtronic, both companies are well-positioned for consistent returns and dividend growth. The piece highlights their resilience and shareholder-friendly policies within the healthcare sector.

feed.title: Inside the Post-Deckers Sanuk Strategy: Rebuilding Wholesale and Reasserting Itself in Surf and Outdoor
feed.summary: Sanuk, under the ownership of Lolë Brands since August 2024, is undergoing a revitalization led by VP and GM Katie Pruitt. The brand aims to expand its consumer base and product assortment, focusing on new leadership hires, updated heritage styles, and successful new products like the Bubblecush sandal. Sanuk is also prioritizing rebuilding its wholesale partnerships and reasserting its presence in the surf and outdoor markets, with a strong focus on connecting with Gen Z consumers through social media.

feed.title: Cooper Companies stock jumps 4% on earnings beat, raised guidance
feed.summary: CooperCompanies (NASDAQ:COO) reported strong first-quarter earnings, surpassing analyst expectations with adjusted EPS of $1.10 against a $1.03 consensus. The medical device company also raised its full-year adjusted EPS guidance to $4.58-$4.66, exceeding the previous consensus of $4.52, and increased its free cash flow guidance. This positive financial performance led to a 4.7% rise in the company's shares.

feed.title: Hasbro Announces Pricing of Public Offering of Notes
feed.summary: Hasbro has announced the pricing of its public offering of notes. This public offering is a common financial strategy used by companies to raise capital. Further details about the offering, such as the total amount, interest rates, and maturity dates, would typically be provided by the company.

feed.title: 'Top request from residents for many years': Port St. Lucie moves closer to landing a Costco retail store
feed.summary: Port St. Lucie is moving closer to landing a Costco retail store, with city leaders set to consider a purchase and sale agreement for 22.8 acres of land in Southern Grove. This comes after residents have requested a Costco for many years. The proposed 170,000-square-foot store and gas station would be adjacent to a Costco distribution depot currently under construction and expected to open this month, employing over 500 people.

feed.title: Amazon Web Outage Strands Customers
feed.summary: Amazon's website and mobile app experienced malfunctions on March 5, with users reporting issues with product listings and checkout options. While Amazon Web Services had reported issues at data centers in the UAE and Bahrain earlier in the week due to drone strikes, an Amazon spokesperson confirmed that AWS was functioning normally during this particular outage, which affected North American retail services. The company acknowledged the problem and was working to resolve it.

feed.title: Texas Roadhouse buys site in 2534 development
feed.summary: Texas Roadhouse has purchased a 2.326-acre site in Johnstown, Colorado for $1.925 million to build an 8,000-square-foot steakhouse. This new restaurant will be located in the 2534 master-planned community, adjacent to a future Boondocks Fun Center, and is expected to bring jobs and dining options to the area. The Town of Johnstown mayor welcomed the development, highlighting Texas Roadhouse's commitment to sustainability and community involvement.

feed.title: Paycom Software authorizes $200M add-on to stock repurchase plan
feed.summary: Paycom Software (PAYC) has authorized an additional $200 million for its stock repurchase plan, significantly adding to the $1.45 billion already repurchased since July 2024. The board-approved extension aims to replenish the existing buyback plan, with repurchases to be made through various channels and depending on market conditions, with the plan set to expire on August 15, 2026.

feed.title: Gabelli Utility Trust Continues Monthly Distributions, Declares Distributions of $0.05 Per Share
feed.summary: The Gabelli Utility Trust (NYSE: GUT) announced the continuation of its fixed monthly cash distributions of $0.05 per share for July, August, and September 2024. While the Fund has a consistent history of monthly distributions since 1999, the Board of Trustees noted that the shares are currently trading at a premium to net asset value, which may not be sustainable. Approximately 99% of the 2024 distributions are expected to be a return of capital.

feed.title: GLGG Stock Fund Price and Chart — NASDAQ:GLGG
feed.summary: The GLGG fund is the Leverage Shares 2X Long GLXY Daily ETF, which aims to provide twice the daily percentage change in Galaxy Digital Inc. Class A (NASDAQ: GLXY) stock price through swap agreements and options. It is designed for short-term tactical use due to its leveraged nature, with returns potentially deviating if held for longer than a single day. The article provides key financial stats, fund composition, and technical analysis indicators for GLGG.

feed.title: Top Analyst Reports for Exxon Mobil, Palantir & AstraZeneca
feed.summary: This article features Zacks' top analyst reports for March 5, 2026, highlighting major stocks like Exxon Mobil, Palantir Technologies, and AstraZeneca, alongside micro-cap companies Gencor Industries and Hour Loop. The reports delve into each company's recent performance, strategic initiatives, financial health, and potential headwinds. Key insights include Exxon Mobil's production growth, Palantir's AI strategy, AstraZeneca's strong drug pipeline, Gencor's balance sheet, and Hour Loop's consumer traction despite liquidity concerns.

feed.title: Is Chevron Corporation (CVX) A Good Stock To Buy?
feed.summary: This article summarizes a bullish thesis on Chevron Corporation (CVX), highlighting its resilience despite volatile crude prices due to steady oil and gas demand. Key points include its strong cash flow, disciplined capital allocation, and the strategic acquisition of a stake in the Stabroek block, which is expected to boost production growth. The company has also demonstrated commitment to shareholder returns through increased dividends, positioning it as an appealing long-term investment.

feed.title: Paycom (NYSE: PAYC) authorizes additional $200 million for share repurchases
feed.summary: Paycom Software, Inc. (NYSE: PAYC) has authorized an additional $200 million for its common stock repurchase program, bringing the total repurchases since July 2024 to approximately $1.45 billion. The company may execute buybacks through various methods including open market transactions and privately negotiated deals, using cash or borrowings. The program is set to expire on August 15, 2026, and its execution will depend on market conditions and other corporate factors as determined by a Board committee.

feed.title: Port St. Lucie poised to bring Costco store to town
feed.summary: Port St. Lucie is finally anticipated to get a Costco store, a long-desired addition by its residents. The city is set to sell 22 acres to Costco Wholesale for the project, which will be located next to Costco's existing distribution depot in Southern Grove. A crucial vote on the land sale by the Governmental Finance Corp. is scheduled for March 9.

feed.title: Costco Earnings Beat Expectations as Membership Model Shines
feed.summary: Costco Wholesale reported fiscal second-quarter earnings of $4.58 per share, exceeding analyst expectations of $4.55 per share. This strong start to 2026 is helping the company's stock recover losses from the previous year, with the earnings beat potentially extending the current rally.

feed.title: The Cooper Companies, Inc. Q1 Income Advances
feed.summary: The Cooper Companies, Inc. (COO) reported an increase in its first-quarter income, with earnings totaling $130.8 million, or $0.66 per share, compared to $104.3 million, or $0.52 per share, in the previous year. Excluding certain items, adjusted earnings were $217.0 million or $1.10 per share. Revenue for the period rose 5.7% to $1.02 billion from $964.7 million last year, and the company provided full-year EPS guidance of $4.58 to $4.66 and revenue guidance of $4.306 billion to $4.346 billion.

feed.title: CooperCompanies Announces First Quarter 2026 Results
feed.summary: CooperCompanies reported strong financial results for the first quarter of fiscal year 2026, with revenue of $1.024 billion and increased GAAP and non-GAAP diluted earnings per share. The company's performance was driven by product launches and strong profitability in both CooperVision and CooperSurgical segments. CooperCompanies also updated its fiscal year 2026 financial guidance, projecting continued revenue growth and increased free cash flow.

feed.title: Cooper Companies (NASDAQ:COO) Releases Q2 2026 Earnings Guidance
feed.summary: Cooper Companies (NASDAQ:COO) has released its Q2 2026 earnings guidance, projecting EPS between $4.58 and $4.66 and revenue of $4.3 billion, significantly exceeding consensus estimates. Despite this strong outlook, the stock declined by about 2.2% to $80.20, though insiders, including CEO Albert G. White III, have increased their holdings. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average target price of $90.36.

feed.title: Aptiv Board of Directors Approves Spin-Off of Versigent
feed.summary: Aptiv's Board of Directors has approved the spin-off of its Electrical Distribution Systems business into a new publicly traded company, Versigent. The spin-off will be effective April 1, 2026, with Aptiv shareholders receiving one ordinary share of Versigent for every three Aptiv shares held as of the March 17, 2026 record date. Versigent shares are expected to trade on the NYSE under the ticker symbol "VGNT".

feed.title: Gevo (NASDAQ:GEVO) Releases Quarterly Earnings Results, Beats Expectations By $0.01 EPS
feed.summary: Gevo (NASDAQ:GEVO) announced its quarterly earnings, reporting an EPS of ($0.02), which beat analysts' estimates by $0.01, and revenue of $45.35 million, surpassing the consensus of $43.52 million. Despite beating expectations, the company remains unprofitable with a negative net margin of 37.33% and negative return on equity. The stock traded down by 1% following the announcement, and recent insider selling, including by the CFO, has been noted, while analysts maintain a "Hold" consensus rating.

feed.title: Cooper Companies (NASDAQ:COO) Issues Quarterly Earnings Results, Beats Expectations By $0.07 EPS
feed.summary: Cooper Companies (NASDAQ:COO) reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst expectations by $0.07 EPS with $1.10 per share and meeting revenue estimates at $1.02 billion. Despite the positive earnings, the stock price experienced a slight decline. Insider purchases by the CEO and other insiders suggest confidence in the company's future performance.

feed.title: Gap’s (NYSE:GAP) Q4 CY2025 Earnings Results: Revenue In Line With Expectations But Stock Drops
feed.summary: Gap (NYSE:GAP) announced its Q4 CY2025 earnings, with revenue of $4.24 billion meeting Wall Street expectations, marking a 2.1% year-on-year increase. However, the company's Q1 CY2026 revenue guidance of $3.51 billion fell slightly below analyst estimates, leading to a 9.5% drop in stock price. Despite hitting EPS and beating EBITDA expectations, investors reacted negatively to the forward-looking revenue projections.

feed.title: RTX Announces Board Resignation and Governance Adjustment
feed.summary: RTX Corporation announced on March 5, 2026, that director James A. Winnefeld Jr. resigned from its Board of Directors, a move that did not stem from any internal dispute. Following his departure, the Board's size was reduced from eleven to ten members, representing a minor governance adjustment. Analysts currently rate RTX stock as a Buy with a $240.00 price target, while TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates it as Outperform, citing improving financial performance despite valuation concerns and near-term headwinds.
